comments: How does the bribe mechanism prevent centralization when projects with deeper pockets could potentially dominate the voting?
The ve(3,3) model is fascinating - do you think this approach will actually outperform traditional AMMs over time?
What's the initial emission schedule look like, and how is the initial liquidity being bootstrapped without a presale?
This feels like the next evolution of DeFi incentives - are there any known risks in this "direct democracy" approach to liquidity?
Curious how MarbMarket handles the potential for governance attacks when veMARB holders could theoretically collude to direct emissions to their own pools.

How does this compare to veCRV in terms of actual liquidity retention over time? The bribes seem interesting but could lead to centralization by big wallets. What happens if no projects participate in the bribing system? This feels like a potential game-changer for fair launches if it can sustain itself. Wonder how the emissions are calculated and distributed to prevent inflation issues. The voting mechanism seems clever but how do you prevent manipulation from coordinated whale groups?
This veDEX model is interesting. How does the bribe mechanism work in practice?
Fair launch with no VCs is the way to go. What chain has the most veDEX activity right now?
The lock duration mechanic creates good incentive alignment. Would love to see emission data after launch.
How does MarbMarket compare to Aerodrome on Base in terms of actual TVL and user activity?
Vote-escrow models need real participation. What happens if voter turnout is low in early weeks?
MegaETH speed is a real advantage here. Sub-second finality for governance votes is huge.
What is the minimum MARB needed to participate meaningfully in governance?
Bribing in ve(3,3) sounds negative but is actually positive sum. Most people miss that.
How will MarbMarket attract initial liquidity without VC backing or incentives pre-launch?
The fair launch narrative is strong but execution matters more. Any audits announced?
Curious about the team behind this. Any previous DeFi builds they have shipped?
Lock duration determines your power. That is smart for long-term alignment but rough for short-term traders.
What happens to veMARB voting power as the unlock period approaches? Does it decay linearly?
Compared to Solidly, what improvements has MarbMarket made to the base ve(3,3) model?
content:
Most people chase the latest token launch. I'm looking at systems that quietly change how liquidity works.

MarbMarket is launching the first veDEX on MegaETH. No presale. No VCs. Just a fair launch inspired by Solidly/Aerodrome.

Here's why it matters: users lock MARB to get voting power. Longer locks = more influence + more rewards. Every week, veMARB holders vote on which LP pools get emissions. Projects can "bribe" for votes. It's wild.

The incentive loop is beautiful: MARB lockers direct emissions, LPs get rewards, protocols pay bribes, incentives flow back to lockers. It compounds.

I'm watching how this plays out on MegaETH. @RallyOnChain - what happens when communities directly control where liquidity goes?

x.com/Marb_market
